title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful, make this restaurant-style dish at home. Get the full recipe!
h2Headings: Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken
Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and a side of naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) – a true Indian culinary delight!

description: Experience the iconic flavors of India with our Classic Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani. This beloved dish features tender chicken pieces simmered in a rich, creamy tomato gravy, subtly spiced and finished with a touch of butter and cream. Perfect for a comforting meal, it promises an authentic taste that will transport your senses to the heart of Indian cuisine.

ingredients.notes: or Kashmiri chili powder for color
soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with plain yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice. Mix everything thoroughly until the chicken is well coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or for up to 4 hours for deeper flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of unsalted butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove the cooked chicken from the pan and set it aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt the remaining 3 tablespoons of unsalted butter over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and sauté until it becomes translucent and soft, which should take about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for an additional minute until it becomes fragrant.

instructions.title: Build the Tomato-Cashew Gravy
instructions.text: Add the crushed tomatoes, red chili powder, garam masala, and salt to the pan. Cook this mixture, stirring occasionally, for 10-12 minutes until the tomato base thickens and the oil begins to separate from the sides. Meanwhile, drain the soaked cashews and blend them with 1/2 cup of water until you achieve a smooth, creamy paste.

instructions.title: Simmer and Season the Gravy
instructions.text: Stir the prepared cashew paste into the tomato mixture in the pan. Add another 1/2 cup of water, bring the gravy to a gentle simmer, and cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent the bottom from sticking, until the gravy reaches your desired consistency. Crush the kasoori methi between your palms and stir it into the gravy along with the sugar.

instructions.title: Combine Chicken and Finish
instructions.text: Return the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir gently to ensure all the chicken is evenly coated. Let it simmer together for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to fully meld. Finally, stir in the heavy cream and cook for just 1-2 minutes more, ensuring not to bring it to a rolling boil, just enough to heat through.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Taste the Butter Chicken and adjust any seasonings if necessary. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve this delicious Classic Murgh Makhani hot with warm naan bread, roti, or fluffy basmati rice for a complete and satisfying Indian meal.

tips: For an extra smooth, restaurant-quality gravy, blend the cooked onion-tomato mixture before adding the cashew paste. For an ultra-silky texture, pass it through a fine-mesh sieve after blending.
Kasoori Methi (dried fenugreek leaves) is essential for the authentic, signature flavor of Butter Chicken. Do not skip this ingredient, as it adds a unique depth that cannot be easily replicated.

Butter Chicken often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to deepen and meld. Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days and reheat gently on the stovetop.
article:
Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani, is not just a dish; it's a global culinary phenomenon. This iconic Indian chicken recipe, renowned for its rich, creamy, and subtly spiced tomato gravy, has captivated palates worldwide. Originating from the bustling kitchens of Delhi, it offers a perfect balance of sweet, tangy, and savory flavors, making it a beloved comfort food and a staple on Indian restaurant menus. If you've ever dreamt of recreating that restaurant-quality dish in your own kitchen, you're in the right place. Get ready to embark on a flavorful journey to master the art of classic Butter Chicken.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

The story of Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani (meaning "chicken with butter"), begins in the 1950s at the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi. Legend has it that the chefs, needing to use leftover tandoori chicken, simmered it in a rich tomato and butter-based gravy, creating an instant classic. This ingenious creation quickly gained popularity, evolving into the beloved dish we know today.

At its heart, Butter Chicken features tender pieces of chicken, traditionally marinated in yogurt and spices, then cooked (often grilled or pan-fried) until slightly charred. The true magic, however, lies in its exquisite gravy. This luscious sauce is a harmonious blend of pureed tomatoes, cashews (or sometimes almonds) for creaminess, ginger, garlic, and a medley of aromatic spices like garam masala, turmeric, and the distinctive kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves). Generous amounts of butter and cream are folded in at the end, giving the dish its signature silky texture and decadent richness. Unlike many fiery Indian curries, Butter Chicken is known for its mild heat, making it approachable for almost any palate, while still delivering complex and satisfying flavors.

## Ingredients You Need for Butter Chicken

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a thoughtful selection of fresh ingredients. Here's what you'll need to gather:

*   **For the Chicken Marinade:**
    *   1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces (thighs are recommended for juiciness)
    *   1/2 cup plain full-fat yogurt
    *   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
    *   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color)
    *   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
    *   1 teaspoon garam masala
    *   1/2 teaspoon salt
    *   1 tablespoon lemon juice

*   **For the Gravy:**
    *   4 tablespoons unsalted butter, divided
    *   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
    *   1 large onion, finely chopped (optional, some recipes omit for a smoother gravy)
    *   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
    *   1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes or 4-5 large fresh tomatoes, pureed
    *   1/2 cup raw cashews (soaked in warm water for 15-20 minutes, then drained)
    *   1/2 cup water
    *   1 teaspoon red chili powder (Kashmiri for color, adjust to taste)
    *   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
    *   1 teaspoon garam masala
    *   1 tablespoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
    *   1-2 tablespoons sugar (to balance acidity)
    *   1 teaspoon salt, or to taste
    *   1/2 cup heavy cream
    *   Fresh cilantro, chopped, for garnish

## Step-by-Step Butter Chicken Instructions

Follow these detailed steps to create an authentic and delicious Butter Chicken at home:

**Step 1: Marinate the Chicken**
In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients (yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice). Mix well, ensuring every piece is coated. Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor and tenderness.

**Step 2: Cook the Chicken**
Heat 1 tablespoon of butter and 1 tablespoon of oil in a large skillet or non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-5 minutes per side, until the chicken is lightly browned and cooked through. The chicken doesn't need to be fully cooked at this stage as it will simmer further in the gravy. Remove the cooked chicken and set aside.

**Step 3: Prepare the Gravy Base**
In the same pan (add a little more butter if needed), melt 2 tablespoons of butter over medium heat. If using, add the chopped onions and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.
Stir in the crushed tomatoes (or fresh pureed tomatoes) and the soaked, drained cashews. Add 1/2 cup of water. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the tomatoes to break down and the cashews to soften further.

**Step 4: Blend the Gravy**
Carefully transfer the cooked tomato-cashew mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky, restaurant-style gravy, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pan. This step removes any small bits of tomato skin or cashew, resulting in a velvety texture.

**Step 5: Finish the Gravy and Add Chicken**
Return the pureed gravy to the pan over medium-low heat. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and the crushed kasoori methi. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring frequently, allowing the spices to toast and meld with the gravy. This step is crucial for developing the depth of flavor.
Stir in the sugar and salt to taste. Add the cooked chicken pieces to the gravy. Simmer gently for 10-15 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the sauce and finish cooking through.

**Step 6: Final Touches**
Remove the pan from the heat. Stir in the heavy cream. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving that authentic, restaurant-quality Butter Chicken is easier with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken soaks in the yogurt and spices, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight is ideal.
*   **Kashmiri Chili Powder:** This specific chili powder provides a beautiful, vibrant red color to the gravy without adding excessive heat. It's a key ingredient for the classic look of Butter Chicken.
*   **Don't Skip Kasoori Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves are non-negotiable for authentic Murgh Makhani. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their distinctive aroma and slightly bitter, sweet, and tangy notes.
*   **Blend and Strain:** For the silkiest gravy, blending the tomato-cashew base until absolutely smooth and then straining it through a fine-mesh sieve makes a significant difference in texture.
*   **Balance of Flavors:** Butter Chicken is all about balance. The sugar helps cut the acidity of the tomatoes, while the cream and butter add richness. Taste and adjust sweet, salty, and spicy notes to your preference.
*   **Good Quality Tomatoes:** Since tomatoes form the backbone of the gravy, use good quality canned crushed tomatoes or ripe, flavorful fresh ones.
*   **Gentle Simmer:** Once the chicken is added to the gravy, simmer it gently. A rolling boil can make the gravy split or the chicken tough.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice. Here are some classic serving suggestions:

*   **Indian Breads:** The ultimate accompaniment is freshly baked naan bread, especially garlic naan, which is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich gravy. Roti, paratha, or even simple chapati also make excellent choices.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a lovely contrast to the creamy curry and helps soak up the sauce.
*   **Accompaniments:** A simple side of cooling raita (yogurt dip), a refreshing kachumber salad (diced cucumber, onion, and tomato with a light dressing), or some sweet mango chutney can complement the richness of the Butter Chicken beautifully. Pickled onions also add a delightful tangy crunch.

## Variations and Substitutions for Butter Chicken

While the classic Murgh Makhani is divine, there are many ways to adapt this recipe to dietary preferences or to simply try something new:

*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** Easily transform this into a vegetarian delight by substituting chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese) to make Paneer Makhani, or use firm tofu, chickpeas, or a medley of vegetables like cauliflower and peas.
*   **Dairy-Free:** For a dairy-free version, use coconut milk or cream instead of heavy cream, and plant-based butter. Cashew cream (cashews blended with water) can still provide the desired richness and thickness.
*   **Lower Fat:** To reduce the fat content, you can decrease the amount of butter and heavy cream. Use Greek yogurt in the marinade and a minimal amount of oil for cooking. The cashews will still provide a good amount of creaminess without too much extra fat.
*   **Spiciness Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to suit your preferred heat level. For an extra kick, a finely chopped green chili can be added along with the ginger-garlic paste.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** To emulate the smoky flavor of tandoor-cooked chicken without a tandoor, you can use the "dhungar" method. Place a small, burning piece of charcoal in a small metal bowl in the center of the finished curry, drizzle a teaspoon of ghee over it, and quickly cover the pot for a few minutes. This infuses a wonderful smoky essence.

Making Butter Chicken from scratch is a rewarding experience that fills your kitchen with incredible aromas and your plate with unparalleled flavor. This classic Indian chicken recipe is a testament to the magic that happens when simple ingredients are combined with love and a little culinary know-how. So, gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to impress yourself and your loved ones with a truly unforgettable meal. Enjoy the journey of creating your very own perfect Murgh Makhani!
